title: dactylorhiza sambucina var. hybrida
intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina var. hybrida, also known as the woodcock orchid, is a rare and endangered hybrid orchid species found in Europe and parts of Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The woodcock orchid is believed to have several health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a diuretic to help with urinary issues. In herbal preparations, the woodcock orchid is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use is largely limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
